segments.text: Judge not, that you be not judged.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For with what judgment you judge, you will be judged; and with the measure you use, it will be measured back to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And why do you look at the speck in your brother's eye, but do not consider the plank in your own eye?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or how can you say to your brother, 'Let me remove the speck from your eye'; and look, a plank is in your own eye?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Hypocrite! First remove the plank from your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother's eye.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Do not give what is holy to the dogs; nor cast your pearls before swine, lest they trample them under their feet, and turn and tear you in pieces.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For everyone who asks receives, and he who seeks finds, and to him who knocks it will be opened.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or what man is there among you who, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or if he asks for a fish, will he give him a serpent?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask Him!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore, whatever you want men to do to you, do also to them, for this is the Law and the Prophets.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Enter by the narrow gate; for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and there are many who go in by it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Because narrow is the gate and difficult is the way which leads to life, and there are few who find it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: You Will Know Them by Their Fruits

segments.text: Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly they are ravenous wolves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You will know them by their fruits. Do men gather grapes from thornbushes or figs from thistles?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Even so, every good tree bears good fruit, but a bad tree bears bad fruit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, nor can a bad tree bear good fruit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore by their fruits you will know them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Not everyone who says to Me, 'Lord, Lord,' shall enter the kingdom of heaven, but he who does the will of My Father in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Many will say to Me in that day, 'Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in Your name, cast out demons in Your name, and done many wonders in Your name?'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I never knew you; depart from Me, you who practice lawlessness!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore whoever hears these sayings of Mine, and does them, I will liken him to a wise man who built his house on the rock:
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and the rain descended, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house; and it did not fall, for it was founded on the rock.
But everyone who hears these sayings of Mine, and does not do them, will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand:
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and the rain descended, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house; and it fell. And great was its fall.
And so it was, when Jesus had ended these sayings, that the people were astonished at His teaching,
for He taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
